domain: Epistemic practices and unpublished tricks of the bbchallenge community (probvious calculus, cryptid taxonomy, holdout management, machine breeding) — reverse-translated into standard mathematics

translations.technique: The 'probvious' calculus: per-machine stochastic models with exact conditional hitting probabilities, updated by simulation burn-in
translations.what_it_actually_does: For each cryptid they first PROVE (often later in Coq/Rocq) an exact Collatz-like rule set, e.g. Hydra: C(2k,0)->Halt, C(2k,b)->C(3k+3,b-1), C(2k+1,b)->C(3k+3,b+2); halting reduces to an auxiliary counter hitting -1. They then model the residue/parity itinerary as i.i.d. uniform and compute the EXACT absorbing-barrier hitting probability under that model: for Hydra's +2/-1 walk, P(hit -1 from n) = (1/phi)^(n+1); for Bigfoot's walk, (1/2)^(n+1). Finally they simulate the real machine for millions of steps purely to certify the counter has reached level N (Hydra: b = 2,005,373 after 4M steps) and quote P(halt) <= (1/phi)^(N+1) ~ 10^-400,000. The BB5 paper prints ((sqrt(5)-1)/2)^1,073,720,885 for Antihydra. Classification labels 'probviously (non)halting' are assigned per machine; crucially they also assign INTERMEDIATE credences (machine 1RB1LE_0LC0LB_1RD1LC_1RD1RA_1RF0LA_---1RE: '3/5 chance of becoming a Translated Cycler, 2/5 chance of halting', Racheline) and curate a failure-mode counterexample: for 1RB0LE_1LC1RA_---1LD_0RB1LF_1RD1LA_0LA0RD the naive i.i.d.-uniform model on residues mod 6 predicts halting w.p. 1, but the reachable set is deterministically confined to residues {0,3} mod 6, avoiding the halting residues {2,4}; mxdys proved it nonhalting in Rocq (Nov 2024).
translations.math_translation: Implicit conditional theorem schema: Let f be a map on N^k with proven rule set R, halting set D, and itinerary map pi(x) in {0,...,m-1}^omega (residues driving rule choice). (1) [Exact, provable] For every n >= 0, if pi(x) ~ mu (i.i.d. uniform, or any Markov measure respecting R's proven congruence constraints), then P_mu(orbit of x hits D | counter currently at n) = h(n), where h is the exact solution of the gambler's-ruin recurrence (e.g. h(n) = ((sqrt(5)-1)/2)^(n+1) for increments {+2,-1} with equal probability). (2) [The single unproven hypothesis, always the same] the actual orbit's itinerary is mu-generic. So 'M probviously does not halt with confidence 1-eps' is precisely the conditional theorem: 'itinerary genericity of the observed orbit implies M's halting event lies in a set of mu-measure <= eps, where eps = h(N) for the certified burn-in level N.' The failure mode is precisely choosing mu that violates a PROVABLE invariant sub-lattice of congruences — i.e., failing to quotient out the structured part before randomizing.

nearest_known_math.theory: Lagarias & Weiss, 'The 3x+1 Problem: Two Stochastic Models', Ann. Appl. Prob. 2 (1992) 329-361 — random walk imitating T mod 2^j plus large deviations
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by (in mechanism): the drift/random-walk model of a Collatz-like map with quantitative predictions is exactly Lagarias-Weiss's RW model, reinvented per machine

nearest_known_math.theory: Terras (1976) density-1 stopping-time theorem; Tao (2019) 'Almost all orbits of the Collatz map attain almost bounded values'
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: these are the rigorous shadows of the same heuristic — genericity of parity itineraries proven for almost-all seeds instead of assumed for one seed

nearest_known_math.theory: Cramér's model for primes and heuristic-calculus traditions (Poonen-style heuristic frameworks, Cohen-Lenstra)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: same epistemic move (structured part exact, residual modeled random), but number theorists rarely quote conditional probabilities for a SINGLE instance
nearest_known_math.ref: Cramér 1936; Poonen, 'Heuristics for the Brauer-Manin obstruction'

nearest_known_math.theory: Algorithmic randomness: Martin-Löf tests and Schnorr supermartingales
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps (unnoticed by the community): the burn-in-conditioned bound h(N) is exactly a supermartingale value certifying the halting event sits in level N of an effective null cover under the model measure
nearest_known_math.ref: Downey & Hirschfeldt, Algorithmic Randomness and Complexity

translations.fusion_opportunity: Fuse with Lagarias-Weiss and verified reductions to define a formal object: a 'heuristic certificate' = (Coq-verified rule set R, model class M = all Markov measures consistent with R's provable congruence lattice, exact hitting bound sup_{mu in M} P_mu(halt | certified prefix) <= eps). This is machine-checkable end-to-end; the only informal residue is the choice of M, and the mod-6 failure case shows how to make M's construction algorithmic (quotient by the maximal provable invariant congruence structure first — Tao's structure-vs-randomness dichotomy as an executable preprocessing step). Result: 'probvious' becomes a precise conditional theorem with an auditable hypothesis, uniformly over a model class rather than one cherry-picked measure.
translations.collatz_application: For Collatz proper this recovers the Terras/Lagarias program (and explains why the heuristic is stuck: for the actual orbit of a single n, genericity IS the conjecture). For our certificate program: (a) emit two-part certificates — verified reduction + worst-case-over-model-class hitting bound — as first-class outputs for machines we cannot decide; (b) adopt the community's debugging discipline: before any drift computation, compute the exact invariant congruence lattice of the map (2-adic/3-adic constraints) and model only the residual degrees of freedom, since our critical-drift-gap statistics are garbage if a deterministic sub-lattice is silently biasing the itinerary distribution.

translations.technique: Champion-beating forecasts: probability distributions over the value of an uncomputable function (Lucy's Moonlight, 'the 5% champion')
translations.what_it_actually_does: Racheline and Shawn Ligocki derived reset-structured rules C(a,b,c) for BB(6) holdout Lucy's Moonlight, computed c0=14, c1=11,292, c2~10^2901.92, then built a checkpoint model: P(reach a=1 from a checkpoint) converges exponentially fast to the fixed point 3/5 of P(n)=1-(2/3)P(n-1); at a=1, halting/reset/zero-phase each get 1/3; net: 1/5 halt, 4/5 reset per checkpoint, so reset count is geometric(1/5). Each reset tetrates the output (r resets -> > 10^^(r+3.5) ones); the reigning champion writes < 10^^15.6 ones, so beating it requires r >= 13 resets, P = (4/5)^13 ~ 5.5%. Conclusion published as: 'Lucy's Moonlight has a 5% chance of being the true BB(6) champion.' The same move at BB(3,3): the 'probvious champion' 1RB2LC1RC_2LC---2RB_2LA0LB0RA 'probabilistically has a 100% chance of halting' and would extend the 10^17 bound if proven.
translations.math_translation: Implicit statement with quantifiers: under the product model mu = prod over holdouts M of mu_M, the busy beaver value Sigma(6) is the random variable max(champion, max_M sigma(M) * 1[M halts under mu_M]); the community computes tail probabilities such as P_mu(Sigma(6) >= 10^^16) ~ 0.055 from geometric reset-count laws composed with tetrational output-scale maps. The exact sub-theorem: if checkpoint outcomes are i.i.d. with the stated conditionals, then (number of resets before halt) ~ Geometric(1/5) and conditional on r resets the halting output exceeds 10^^(r+3.5). Note the probability arithmetic is forced onto the log-log (tetration-index) scale — the model's randomness only matters through an integer-valued 'height' variable.

nearest_known_math.theory: Lagarias-Weiss extremal-constant predictions: their stochastic models predict lim sup sigma_infty(n)/log n = gamma_0 ~ 41.677647 for 3x+1, matching data to 10^11 — literally a 'champion forecast' via large deviations/extreme value theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by in spirit: forecasting the extremal statistic of an arithmetic dynamical ensemble from a stochastic model is their program; bbchallenge does it for the BB record over a FINITE explicit ensemble of machines

nearest_known_math.theory: Chaitin's Omega / algorithmic probability (halting probability of a universal machine)
nearest_known_math.relationship: disjoint-from in mechanism (Omega weights by program length, not dynamics), but it is the only prior art assigning probabilities to halting events of specific machines

nearest_known_math.theory: Extreme value theory of record processes (Gumbel; records of heavy-tailed sequences)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: the BB record over holdouts is a record process over wildly heavy-tailed (tetrational) score distributions; nobody in EVT handles tetration-scale tails
nearest_known_math.ref: Feller Vol II; Resnick, Extreme Values

translations.fusion_opportunity: Formalize 'conditional busy beaver distributions': fix a model-class assignment M -> mu_M for every holdout (auditable, as in technique 1) and define BB_mu(6) as a genuine random variable; then EVT over the holdout ensemble gives a posterior record distribution whose tail is dominated by near-critical-drift machines. The mathematically clean fusion object: since gambler's-ruin excursion scales blow up like exp(c/|drift|) as drift -> 0, the record tail of BB_mu is governed by the empirical density of holdouts near zero drift — an explicit, testable link between a population statistic (drift spectrum of the machine ensemble) and the forecast of a single uncomputable integer.
translations.collatz_application: Directly imports to our critical-drift-gap experiment: for bred families of Collatz-like maps, the community's practice predicts stopping-time records among near-critical members scale like exp(Theta(1/|drift|)) with geometric 'reset' counts controlling the tetration height — a quantitative null hypothesis our experiment can confirm or refute. Also a warning: their probabilities are model tail-bounds, not calibrated credences; if we publish 'confidence' numbers for Collatz-adjacent claims we should publish the model class alongside, as they now (post-BB5-paper footnote 23) implicitly do.

translations.technique: Wall arguments with backward-reachability pruning (the BB(3,3) 'probvious champion' analysis, LegionMammal)
translations.what_it_actually_does: For 1RB2LC1RC_2LC---2RB_2LA0LB0RA they did BOTH directions: backward, they enumerated the halting basin exactly — only 64 halting c-values up to 3.4x10^15, organized into 'halter families' (infinite parametrized sets), and showed the first 58 are unreachable by forward iteration; then they found one halting value H = (3^307 - 8282708212)/13959275 with a 'wall': every A(1,c) with c <= H - 3,403,055 flows into H, i.e. a certified interval of >3.4 million consecutive attracted values. Forward, the orbit's c-values grow by pseudorandom chains whose lengths are empirically geometric with mean ~ log_3(c) + const. Jumping over a 3.4M-wide wall would need a chain of length ~10^1000-scale improbability, so the machine 'probabilistically has a 100% chance of halting' (BB5 paper's phrasing) — a probviously HALTING cryptid, which if proven becomes the BB(3,3) champion.
translations.math_translation: Hybrid exact/stochastic theorem: Let f be the induced map on c-values, D the halting basin. (i) [exact, certified] D contains an interval I of length L = 3,403,055 immediately below H; (ii) [exact] forward orbits are monotone-increasing between rare renewal events, with jump sizes J_k; (iii) [model] if J_k are independent geometric with mean m(c) = log_3 c + O(1), then P(the increasing orbit ever leaps over an interval of length L below its running position) <= sum_k P(J_k > L) ~ exp(-cL/m), so P(halt) = 1 - o(1). Quantifier form: for all monotone processes with certified sub-basin interval of length L and jump law stochastically dominated by Geometric(p), P(miss I forever) <= exp(-pL(1-o(1))). The structure — exact backward basin computation composed with a forward stochastic hitting estimate on the residual — is Lagarias-Weiss's two models (forward RW mod 2^j, backward branching RW mod 3^j) fused into a single argument about one orbit.

nearest_known_math.theory: Renewal theory / first-passage and interval-hitting for random walks (Feller); ladder epochs
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by: the wall-jump estimate is a union bound over renewal jumps, textbook probability
nearest_known_math.ref: Feller, An Introduction to Probability Theory, Vol I-II

nearest_known_math.theory: Wirsching's analysis of the Collatz predecessor tree (branching structure of T^{-1}); Lagarias-Weiss backward branching random walk
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: exact backward-reachability over the preimage tree is the rigorous half of their program; bbchallenge runs it as a finite computation ('halter families', 58 of 64 values pruned)
nearest_known_math.ref: Wirsching, LNM 1681, 'The Dynamical System Generated by the 3n+1 Function'

nearest_known_math.theory: Covering-congruence + heuristic hybrid arguments in number theory (Erdős-style: exact algebraic pruning, then density heuristics on the residue)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps in epistemic shape

translations.fusion_opportunity: Package the hybrid as a reusable certified lemma: 'certified basin interval of length L' + 'certified stochastic domination of jump law by Geometric(p)' => quantitative model-halting bound 1 - exp(-pL). The first two components are individually provable (the basin interval by finite computation, the domination sometimes provable from the rule set's structure); this would make wall arguments the FIRST probvious arguments where every input to the probability calculation is a theorem, leaving only itinerary independence as hypothesis. No one in the community has isolated this; no one in the Collatz literature composes exact finite basin computations with domination lemmas this way.
translations.collatz_application: The Collatz verified range n <= 2^68 IS a wall: any orbit dipping below its start enters certified territory and dies, so divergent orbits must be monotone-in-records above 2^68 — the community's formulation suggests certifying 'wall-width vs jump-law' inequalities as lemmas in our certificate program: compute exact preimage-tree coverage below a threshold (Wirsching-style), prove a jump-law domination for the residual map, and emit a quantitative conditional halting bound. For bred families near critical drift, wall arguments are the natural certificate for the probviously-halting side of the gap.

translations.technique: Behavioral-equivalence quotients: certified orbit-merging, TNF canonicalization, and cross-substrate cryptid identification
translations.what_it_actually_does: Three distinct layers operate in practice. (i) Syntactic: Tree Normal Form quotients machine space by permutations of non-start states/symbols/directions, normalizes unused transitions to '---' and halts to 1RZ (plus the TNF-1RB refinement). (ii) Orbit-merging: holdout #758 was shown equivalent to holdout #153 because after ~1.8M steps its configuration equals a configuration of #153 up to state/symbol relabeling — identical evolution thereafter; mxdys's grouping cut BB(6) holdout equivalence classes from 2467 to 1691 (31%), and the canonical list is now maintained 'up to equivalence' (1094 classes vs 2320 machines, June 2026). (iii) Abstract conjugacy: Hydra (BB(2,5)) and Antihydra (BB(6)) provably reduce to the SAME map H(n) = floor(3n/2) with different seeds (3 vs 8) and dual halting conditions (E > 2O vs O > 2E); Racheline's 'Collatz Coincidence' (Sept 2024) found by hand-comparing trajectory sequences that the 1989 S(5) champion, S(5) runner-up, BBB(4) champion, and S(2,4) champion all simulate one Collatz-like map up to affine conjugacy (B(n) -> M(2n)).
translations.math_translation: Layer (ii), the load-bearing one: define M1 ~ M2 iff there exist t1, t2 in N and a bijection sigma on (states x symbols) (optionally with tape reflection) such that sigma(conf_{M1}(t1)) = conf_{M2}(t2). Trivial theorem: ~ implies halting statuses from blank tape coincide (and step counts differ by t1 - t2). The relation is Sigma_1 (semi-decidable); the community computes explicit finite certificates (t1, t2, sigma) at population scale and quotients its problem lists by them. Layer (iii): machines are 'the same cryptid' iff their verified abstractions (f_i, seed x_i, halting set D_i) are conjugate by an affine map phi on the parameter lattice with phi(D_1) = D_2 (or the order-dual condition, as with Hydra/Antihydra where the halting inequality is reversed) — i.e., commensurability of Collatz-like maps up to affine change of variables and duality.

nearest_known_math.theory: Conjugacy and shift equivalence in symbolic dynamics (Lind & Marcus); orbit equivalence
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: layer (iii) is affine conjugacy of induced maps, standard; but layer (ii) — literal orbit MERGING of two different systems after finite time in a common configuration space — is stronger than conjugacy and is not a standard studied relation
nearest_known_math.ref: Lind & Marcus, An Introduction to Symbolic Dynamics and Coding

nearest_known_math.theory: Bisimulation and weak bisimulation (Milner, Park) in process theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: halting-status-preserving behavioral equivalence with finite certificates is bisimulation-flavored, but orbit-merging is a single-trace, not branching, notion

nearest_known_math.theory: Classification of generalized 3x+1 maps up to linear conjugacy (Wirsching; Matthews's generalized Collatz surveys)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the abstract layer: affine-conjugacy classification of periodic-linear maps is established
nearest_known_math.ref: Wirsching LNM 1681; K. Matthews, generalized 3x+1 mappings survey

nearest_known_math.theory: Michel 1993, 'Busy beaver competition and Collatz-like problems' (local PDF)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the discovery that champions simulate Collatz-like maps — published 1993; the community's addition is recognizing the SAME map across substrates, which Michel did not do

translations.fusion_opportunity: The one genuinely under-theorized piece is certified orbit-merging as a quotient on POPULATIONS of dynamical systems: a semi-decidable equivalence with explicit finite witnesses, used so that any proof about one representative transfers to the class. Fusing with symbolic dynamics: study the 'merge graph' on machine space (edges = orbit-merge certificates) as an object — its components measure redundancy of the enumeration, and merge-time (1.8M steps for #758/#153) is a new conjugacy-like invariant with no classical analogue. A dynamicist would recognize everything else here; merge-time distributions across TNF space they would not.
translations.collatz_application: Before running certificate searches over bred Collatz-like families, canonicalize by affine conjugacy + duality (Hydra/Antihydra teach that one theorem about H(n) = floor(3n/2) parity statistics simultaneously resolves machines in BB(2,5) and BB(6)); and de-duplicate by orbit-merging, or the critical-drift-gap statistics will double-count (bbchallenge's own numbers: 2320 nominal problems = 1094 real ones — a factor 2.1 that would corrupt any population-level drift census).

translations.technique: Cryptid breeding via TNF-tree adjacency: the 'Mother of Giants' pattern of one-cylinder perturbation families
translations.what_it_actually_does: Take a machine with one undefined transition — the Mother, 1RB1LE_0LC0LB_0LD1LC_1RD1RA_---0LA, which itself halts in 58 steps — and consider all 'children' obtained by filling the E0 slot. ALL children provably share a common Collatz-like rule set on F(a,b,c) = 0^inf <D 0^a 1 0^b 1^c 0^inf: F(a,b+2,c) -> F(a+7,b,c); F(a,1,c+1) -> F(a+6,0,c); F(a,0,1) -> F(1,0,a+3); F(a,0,0) -> quasihalt. They differ ONLY in how the exceptional configuration F(2k,0,c+2) resolves: E0->1RC/1LC gives F(1,k+3,c+1); E0->1RD gives F(7,k-1,c+1); etc. The children's quasihalting times range from ~10^4079 through ~10^12978 to >10^14006 (the current BBB(5) champion) — tetration-scale sensitivity to a single rule. The community explicitly frames this as exploring 'parameterizations of these 2-stage style Collatz-like functions' and tracks which parameter choices give exceptional orbits. The same pattern generates variant cryptids (a dozen 6-state cryptids, several Antihydra variants; Coq-BB5's constructed BB(3,3)-in-BB(7) compilations).
translations.math_translation: Definition (implicit): a breeding family is {f_theta : theta in Theta} of maps on N^k agreeing outside one congruence-defined cylinder C, with f_theta|_C ranging over a finite set of affine maps indexed by the filled transition. Empirical theorem (with quantifiers): there exists a single proven rule set R and cylinder C such that for all theta, f_theta satisfies R off C, yet the quasihalting-time functional T(f_theta) varies over a range exceeding 10^10000 — i.e., sup_theta log T / inf_theta log T > 10^3 — while all members remain 'probviously quasihalting'. The drift-determining skeleton is shared; the exceptional rule alone selects the excursion scale.

nearest_known_math.theory: Conway's unpredictable iterations (1972) / FRACTRAN; Kurtz & Simon, 'The Undecidability of the Generalized Collatz Problem' (Pi^0_2-completeness)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: explains WHY one-rule perturbations can do anything (the family of Collatz-like maps is a universal programming substrate), but says nothing about the statistics of stopping times across a structured family

nearest_known_math.theory: One-parameter families in interval dynamics / kneading theory / bifurcation theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: disjoint-from in technique: continuous parameters, observables are entropy and attractors, not stopping times of integer orbits

nearest_known_math.theory: Lagarias-Weiss / Kontorovich-Lagarias stochastic models for qx+1 families (3x+1 vs 5x+1 drift sign flip)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: the qx+1 family is the classical drift-interpolating family (3x+1 contracts, 5x+1 expands), but it is a coarse 1-parameter family; nothing in that literature studies one-cylinder perturbations with fixed skeleton
nearest_known_math.ref: Kontorovich & Lagarias, 'Stochastic Models for the 3x+1 and 5x+1 Problems', arXiv:0910.1944

translations.fusion_opportunity: I searched for established work on stopping-time statistics across families of Collatz-like maps that differ on a single congruence class and found none (Kurtz-Simon gives undecidability of the family, Kontorovich-Lagarias only the coarse qx+1 axis). Fusion: treat the filled transition as a discrete parameter sweeping the exceptional-rule contribution to the mean drift, and prove/measure a scaling law T(f_theta) ~ exp(c / |drift(theta)|) as the family crosses criticality — importing the gambler's-ruin excursion asymptotics from technique 1 into the breeding protocol. The Mother-of-Giants data (10^4079 vs 10^14006 among siblings) is existing empirical evidence for exactly this blow-up that nobody has fit a scaling law to.
translations.collatz_application: This is a ready-made protocol for our critical-drift-gap experiment: freeze a verified rule skeleton that pins the bulk drift, and vary one exceptional rule (one congruence class) to interpolate drift through zero — exactly what TNF-tree children do for free. Deliverables: (a) the empirical distribution of stopping times vs drift across the family, (b) certificate-size growth along the same axis (do FAR/CTL certificates blow up polynomially or exponentially as drift -> 0?), (c) whether 'probviously halting' and 'probviously nonhalting' siblings are separated by a genuine gap or a crossover — the community has the specimens but has never plotted the phase diagram.
sources_read: web search results on Kurtz-Simon and Kontorovich-Lagarias (calibration)

translations.technique: N-gram entropy as a provability barometer: empirical entropy of the head-window process predicts decider success (sligocki, forum Heuristics thread #119)
translations.what_it_actually_does: For each machine, sample the process (TM state, 5-symbol tape window centered at the head) along the run and estimate three empirical entropy rates: average entropy increase per symbol, h1 (state + current symbol), h5 (state + window). Findings across populations: 20 machines proven nonhalting by CPS (Closed Position Set, a regular-language decider) with block size <= 6 have median entropy ~0.1; Skelet's list and random BB(5)-era holdouts range 0.06-0.70; the top-100 halting 6-state machines have the lowest entropies (0.026-0.311) because 'they spend 99.99% of their time doing some basic bouncer behavior'. Explicit inference drawn in the thread: 'CPS is taking advantage of lower entropy to create a limited model' — i.e., low measured entropy predicts that a small finite-automaton certificate exists, and high entropy flags machines needing non-regular methods (or cryptid status). Code is on sligocki's GitHub.
translations.math_translation: A CPS/FAR/CTL nonhalting certificate is a regular language L (n-state automaton) with: initial configuration in L; L closed under the TM step relation; L disjoint from halting configurations. Implicit theorem (the necessary direction, provable): if M admits an n-state certificate, then the closure of M's reachable configuration set lies in a sofic shift with an n-state cover, so the topological entropy of the reachable subshift — and hence the entropy rate of any factor process, including the (state, window) process — is at most log(n) (per window normalization). The community uses the CONVERSE empirically: measured entropy h implies 'certificate needs >= exp(h * window) states', treating a necessary condition as a predictive sufficient statistic. Quantified conjecture implicit in the thread: over the enumerated machine population, P(regular certificate with n <= N states exists | empirical h5 = h) is a sharply decreasing function of h with a threshold near h ~ 0.1-0.3.

nearest_known_math.theory: Topological entropy of Turing machines as dynamical systems (Kůrka's TM dynamical topologies; Blondel-Cassaigne-Nichitiu; Jeandel on computability of TM entropy)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the quantity: TM entropy is established and its computability limits are known; NOT established is any link to decider/certificate success
nearest_known_math.ref: Kůrka, 'On topological dynamics of Turing machines', TCS 174 (1997); Jeandel, STACS 2014

nearest_known_math.theory: Sofic approximation and entropy of sofic shifts (entropy of an n-state sofic cover <= log n)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the necessary direction — it is a textbook inequality once the certificate is recognized as a sofic cover

nearest_known_math.theory: Empirical entropy estimation of symbolic sequences (Lempel-Ziv / block-entropy estimators)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the estimator itself
nearest_known_math.ref: Cover & Thomas, Elements of Information Theory

translations.fusion_opportunity: The fusion is to make the converse precise: define cert(M) = min states of a regular closed invariant separator, and prove/refute two-sided bounds cert(M) >= exp(c1 * h(M)) (easy, sofic-cover inequality) and cert(M) <= exp(c2 * h(M) + o(1)) for machines in 'equilibrium-like' classes (hard, probably false in general — Skelet 17 should be the counterexample: low entropy, no small regular certificate, needed a bespoke proof). That failure analysis would itself be a publishable result: WHICH low-entropy machines defeat regular-language methods characterizes the boundary between sofic-approximable and inherently non-regular TM dynamics — a question symbolic dynamics has the tools for but has never asked about certificates.
translations.collatz_application: Collatz parity itineraries are conjecturally normal (entropy ~1 bit/step), which via the sofic-cover inequality gives a clean, previously-unarticulated statement: no regular-language certificate of any subexponential size can decide Collatz-like behavior for maps whose itinerary process has positive entropy — explaining in one line why FAR-style methods dispatch bouncers/counters but never cryptids. For our program: measure h5-analogues (block entropies of orbit encodings) across bred families as the cheap triage statistic — the critical-drift-gap should manifest as an entropy jump, and entropy estimates tell us where certificate search is a priori hopeless.
sources_read: /Users/simon/Desktop/COLLATZ/papers/bbchallenge-2025-bb5-determination.pdf (Sec 7 Zoology, FAR/CTL description around line 1145 of extracted text)

translations.technique: Verified-reduction factorization and problem export: the Beaver Math Olympiad pipeline (halting problem = Coq-checked reduction ∘ named open conjecture)
translations.what_it_actually_does: For each cryptid the community proves — increasingly in Coq/Rocq or Lean — the exact equivalence 'M halts from blank tape iff arithmetic statement phi', then names and exports phi stripped of all TM detail. Concretely: the BB5 paper states Conjecture 1.1 (Antihydra: iterating H(x)=floor(3x/2) from 8 never accumulates more than twice as many odd as even values) as a first-class mathematical conjecture extracted from the machine; the rules of a near-variant are proven in Coq (busycoq AntiHydra2.v, footnote 66); the Beaver Math Olympiad page curates ~8 such problems as olympiad-style sequence questions (e.g. BMO1: (a1,b1)=(1,2), (a_{n+1},b_{n+1}) = (a_n - b_n, 4b_n + 2) if a_n >= b_n else (2a_n + 1, b_n - a_n); does a_i = b_i ever?), now formalized in Lean and merged into DeepMind's formal-conjectures dataset. Three BMO problems have been solved (e.g. Problem 4 by a mod-3 closed form), retiring their machines from holdout lists.
translations.math_translation: For every cryptid M: produce a Collatz-like map f on N^k, seed x0, target set D, and a machine-checked proof of the biconditional [M halts from 0^inf] <-> [exists n: f^n(x0) in D]. This is a verified many-one reduction between two Sigma_1 sentences, with the abstraction chosen to minimize human description length. The epistemic content: the halting problem is FACTORED into a certified part (the reduction, which carries all the TM-specific work and is never again disputed) and a portable open kernel (a pure statement about integer sequences), and problem difficulty is then assessed, traded, and attacked entirely at the kernel level.

nearest_known_math.theory: Coq Library of Undecidability Proofs (Forster, Larchey-Wendling, et al., ITP 2019+): verified many-one reductions between seed problems
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by in mechanism: mechanized reductions between halting-type problems are established practice in certified CS

nearest_known_math.theory: Michel 1993 (and Michel 2015, local PDFs): champions' halting problems are Collatz-like problems
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by in content: the reduction-to-Collatz observation is 30+ years old; the community added mechanization and systematic export

nearest_known_math.theory: Hilbert-problem / olympiad-style problem curation (e.g., Erdős problem lists); formal-conjectures benchmark datasets
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps: problem curation is old sociology, but curating machine-generated open problems WITH certified provenance of their difficulty (each is exactly equivalent to a concrete undecided instance) is new practice

translations.fusion_opportunity: The exportable idea is 'certified provenance of hardness': each BMO problem comes with a proof that solving it settles a specific frontier instance (a BB holdout), so the problem list is guaranteed non-decorative — every solution mechanically advances a global program. Fusing with the equivalence quotient (technique 4): the right export unit is the conjugacy class of the abstract map, not the machine — one kernel theorem about H(n)=floor(3n/2) retires machines in BB(2,5) AND BB(6) simultaneously. A formal 'kernel registry' (abstract maps up to affine conjugacy, each with its list of certified TM reductions) does not exist yet in either community.
translations.collatz_application: Our certificate program should emit exactly this factorization: every certificate = (mechanized reduction to a canonical abstract map, chosen as the affine-conjugacy representative) + (certificate or open conjecture about that map). Benefits: cross-instance transfer for free, and our critical-drift-gap families become exportable as a graded conjecture benchmark (each family member a named sequence problem with certified drift parameter) — the BMO pipeline shows this export path is socially and technically viable, including Lean formalization feeding AI-benchmark datasets.

weird_unnoticed: The probvious confidence numbers are COMPUTE-INDEXED, and nobody says so. Every quoted probability — Hydra's (1/phi)^2,005,374, Antihydra's ((sqrt5-1)/2)^1,073,720,885 ≈ 4.84x10^-224,394,395 in the BB5 paper — is an exact gambler's-ruin hitting probability CONDITIONED on the simulated prefix: they run the machine for millions of steps specifically to push the counter to level N, then read off h(N). So 'confidence' is a monotone function of compute spent, an anytime certificate that mechanically strengthens with every simulated step; structurally it is a supermartingale value, i.e. the halting event is being placed ever deeper inside a Martin-Löf-style effective null cover under the model measure — an algorithmic-randomness object the community has reinvented without recognizing it. Weirder still, the community treats -log P(halt) as an informal invariant of the machine (cryptids are compared by exponent order: 10^-400,000 vs 10^-224 million), effectively using model tail-mass as a 'distance-to-proof' metric, while simultaneously curating its own calibration counterexample (the mod-6 machine where the naive i.i.d. model gives P(halt)=1 but the reachable set provably avoids the halting residues — footnote 23 of the BB5 paper). That pairing — a quantitative confidence currency PLUS a maintained counterexample showing the exact failure mode (forgetting to quotient by the provable invariant congruence lattice before randomizing) — is Tao's structure-vs-randomness dichotomy operating as a community debugging practice, and no summary, wiki page, or paper articulates it.
dead_ends: (1) Discord is where the real methods threads live (mxdys's equivalence-grouping algorithm, cryptid classification debates, the 50 Random Holdouts initiative) — inaccessible from this network; wiki pages repeatedly link into Discord for the actual derivations, so mxdys's 2467->1691 holdout-grouping algorithm is documented only by its input/output sizes, and I could not extract its exact equivalence relation beyond the certified orbit-merge examples. (2) docs.bbchallenge.org unreachable as warned. (3) discuss.bbchallenge.org is largely a fossil: the Heuristics category has only 3-4 topics (the N-gram entropy thread was the sole substantive find); category URLs by guessed ID returned wrong categories until I used categories.json. (4) The BB(6) annotated holdout Google spreadsheet is referenced everywhere but not directly fetchable (no stable public URL found; wiki describes it secondhand). (5) The Probviously wiki page itself is much thinner than the task brief hoped — no explicit confidence taxonomy exists anywhere; the quantitative calculus lives scattered across per-machine wiki pages, sligocki's blog, and Section 1.2/Appendix C of the BB5 paper, and had to be reassembled. (6) Did not clone Coq-BB5/busycoq to read AntiHydra2.v or the RWLAcc/FAR Rocq sources — decider internals belong to a different domain slice and web+PDF evidence sufficed for the epistemic-practice claims; noting this as unverified-at-source for the Coq formalization details.
